source: British Council–EAQUALS Core Inventory for General English, 2nd ed. 2015

cards.title: Full narrative
cards.goal: Can tell a story or describe an experience as a connected, structured narrative with background, events and a point.
grammar: past simple + past continuous woven together
discourse: sequential linkers extended: later, after that, finally, while, as soon as
cards.evidence: Tell a 2–3 minute story with a clear beginning, complication and ending; listener retells the main events in order.
skin_examples: the night everything went wrong at a gig

cards.title: Before the past
cards.goal: Can make the order of past events explicit when narrating, including events that happened earlier.
cards.evidence: Retell a story that needs at least three past perfect clauses to make the sequence clear.
skin_examples: why the set crashed (what had been forgotten)

cards.title: Past habits
cards.goal: Can describe how life used to be, contrasting repeated past habits with the present.
grammar: never used to / didn't use to
cards.evidence: 90-second 'when I was younger' monologue mixing used to (states + habits) and would (habits only), with at least one correct negative.

cards.title: Finished vs unfinished time
cards.goal: Can talk about experiences and recent events, correctly switching between present perfect and past simple.
grammar: present perfect vs past simple (full contrast)
cards.evidence: Interview exchange: five experience questions, each answer opening in present perfect and continuing in past simple with details.

cards.title: Duration up to now
cards.goal: Can say how long a situation or activity has been going on.
grammar: contrast with present perfect simple for results
cards.evidence: Answer how-long questions about five areas of own life, choosing continuous vs simple appropriately.

cards.title: Reported speech
cards.goal: Can report what someone said or asked.
grammar: reported speech across a range of tenses
cards.evidence: Report a short dialogue to a third person: two statements, one question and one explanation, with correct backshift and say/tell distinction.

cards.title: Simple passive
cards.goal: Can describe processes, events and general facts where the agent is unknown or unimportant.
cards.evidence: Describe a process or report an event in six sentences, at least four passive, defending one active/passive choice when asked.

cards.title: Opinions with reasons
cards.goal: Can express personal views and opinions on familiar topics and support them.
cards.evidence: One-minute opinion on a familiar topic with two distinct supporting reasons and one example.

cards.title: Agreeing & disagreeing politely
cards.goal: Can agree and disagree with others, including softened disagreement, and hold his/her position in informal discussion.
exponents: So do I / Neither do I
I see what you mean, but…
I'm sorry, but I think you're wrong
cards.evidence: Ten-statement reaction round using at least six different exponents, including two softened disagreements; defend one disagreement for 30 seconds.

cards.title: Reviewing & recommending
cards.goal: Can summarise a film, book, show or piece of media and give an opinion about it with a recommendation.
exponents: If you like X you'll like this
cards.evidence: 90-second spoken review (what it is, what it's about, what worked, who should watch/hear it), then answer two detail questions.

cards.title: Linking ideas: cause, effect, contrast
cards.goal: Can connect a series of points into linear, linked discourse using explicit logical markers.
discourse: however, therefore, on the other hand
on the one hand… on the other hand…
cards.evidence: Present both sides of a familiar dilemma in one minute using on-the-one-hand/on-the-other-hand plus two other logical markers, concluding with therefore/so.
skin_examples: working for yourself vs a boss

cards.title: Imagining differently (present/future)
cards.goal: Can talk about hypothetical or unlikely present and future situations.
cards.evidence: Answer three what-would-you-do-if questions with full conditional sentences, then pose two of your own.
skin_examples: if you could play any venue
if the bar had unlimited budget

cards.title: Regrets & alternate pasts
cards.goal: Can speculate about how past events could have been different.
cards.evidence: Retell a real past decision and give two third-conditional alternatives.

cards.title: Deduction about the present
cards.goal: Can draw logical conclusions about present situations from evidence.
grammar: contrast with might/may/could for weaker certainty
cards.evidence: Given five clues, produce deductions graded correctly across must be / might be / can't be.

cards.title: Judging & speculating about the past
cards.goal: Can criticise past actions and speculate about what may have happened.
cards.evidence: React to three what-went-wrong scenarios with one should-have judgment and one might-have speculation each.

cards.title: Opening & closing conversations
cards.goal: Can initiate a conversation naturally and end it politely.
exponents: Excuse me, I wonder if you could help me
You'll never believe what I saw…
It's been nice talking to you
Sorry, I've got to go — see you later
cards.evidence: Three cold-start roleplays (stranger, acquaintance, service situation): open, sustain for one minute, close appropriately.

cards.title: Keeping the conversation alive
cards.goal: Can interrupt politely, change the topic, return to a dropped topic, and keep a conversation flowing with natural spoken markers.
functions: managing interaction: interrupting, changing topic, resuming
discourse: informal markers: Right. Really? Well, anyway… I know how you feel
cards.evidence: Five-minute genuine conversation in which the student must interrupt once, change topic once and steer back once, reacting with at least four informal markers (teacher tallies).
skin_examples: any free conversation — skins itself with the week's topic

cards.title: Checking & clarifying
cards.goal: Can check understanding in both directions and repair breakdowns without abandoning the message.
exponents: I'm not sure I understand — are you saying that…?
cards.evidence: Deliberate-obstacle task: reach full understanding of a slightly-too-complex explanation using at least three different clarification moves, then explain it back.

cards.title: Problems & non-routine situations
cards.goal: Can explain why something is a problem and deal with less routine situations (complaints, changes, obstacles) in shops, travel and services.
lexis: travel and services upgraded: check in, departure lounge, debit or credit card
cards.evidence: Two escalation roleplays (wrong booking; faulty purchase): state the problem, explain why it matters, propose a resolution, respond to pushback.

cards.title: Explaining how
cards.goal: Can describe how to do something, giving detailed step-by-step instructions.
discourse: instructional sequencing: first, next, once you've…, make sure…
cards.evidence: Teach the listener a real skill in 2–3 minutes; success measured by whether the listener could actually do it.

cards.title: Refining the future
cards.goal: Can make predictions with graded confidence and describe what will be in progress at a future time.
grammar: will vs going to for prediction (consolidated)
cards.evidence: This-time-next-year monologue: three graded predictions (certain/probable/possible, each marked linguistically) plus two future continuous sentences.
